dialogueChunks.intro: {target_npc} spoke to me, {player_name}. Really spoke to me, for the first time in years.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They didn't forgive me. They said forgiveness might come someday, or it might not, and they won't promise what they can't guarantee. But they thanked me for the rescue and for finally telling the truth. They're coming back to {location}, not for me, but because this is their home and they're done letting someone else's mistake dictate their life. The council agreed to restore their standing. As for me, I've been assigned the worst duties in the settlement for the next year. I've never been happier to shovel manure.
dialogueChunks.outro: You carried my burden to a place I couldn't reach alone. Take this: I don't have much left, but you've earned all of it.

playerResponses.text: Forgiveness on their terms, not yours. That's how it should be.
Manure duty is a small price for a clear conscience. Good luck.

dialogueChunks.intro: It's done, {player_name}. Not the way I dreamed it, but done.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} accepted the {quest_item} and acknowledged what I tried to do. They said they can't live in {location} anymore: too many memories, too much pain in every familiar face. But they shook my hand before they left and said they'd carry one fewer grudge on the road. The settlement knows the truth now, and they're watching me with new eyes: some with pity, some with contempt, some with something that looks like respect for finally owning my failure.
dialogueChunks.outro: I didn't get the ending I wanted. But I got the one I deserved, and it's lighter than the secret was. Here, take this.

playerResponses.text: Owning your failures is the beginning of earning trust back.
A handshake and an honest goodbye. That's worth more than most people get.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}... {target_npc} left a letter. They were gone before I arrived.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The letter said they'd already forgiven me, months ago, alone in the dark one night when the anger finally burned out. They said holding a grudge was heavier than the original wound and they couldn't carry both. But they also said they couldn't face me: not out of anger, but because seeing me would mean reliving the worst day of their life. They wished me peace and asked me not to follow. I'm standing in an empty camp, reading a farewell from someone I wronged, and I feel lighter and emptier than I've ever felt.
dialogueChunks.outro: Forgiveness doesn't always look like a reunion. Sometimes it's a letter and an empty chair. Take this, {player_name}. You helped me reach an answer, even if it wasn't the one I expected.

playerResponses.text: Forgiveness given freely, even from a distance, is still forgiveness.
They let go of the burden. Now you have to let go of yours.
